name: Sling3768Bug
description:
Issue #3768 demo. Composes a multi-cycle substrate (Hook+Agent+HandoffCycle+Mailbox+Pane all looping) with three Patrol+Sling+Bead lanes contending for a shared AssigneeLock. The substrate fires PreCompact maxCycles times; each Resumed window enables a Patrol to invoke its Sling; each Sling acquires the lock, hooks its Bead, releases the lock.

Formula identity (defense-doc P2.5): three Beads with two distinct formulas:
  - Bead1 (formulaId=1) and Bead2 (formulaId=1): SAME formula. Two of these in Hooked status violates the per-formula singleton.
  - Bead3 (formulaId=2): different formula. Even if Hooked, doesn't violate.
The HookedSingleton invariant is pairwise, formula-keyed: 'no two beads of the SAME formula are both Hooked.'

Defense-doc disposition:
  - P1.1 (fan-out): each Sling targets its own Bead via point-to-point binding; no fan-out.
  - P1.2 (lock contention): AssigneeLock has 3 clients; all three Slings contend.
  - P1.3 (baked initial state): no prior bead. All hooks observable in the trace.
  - P2.4 (multi-cycle): substrate loops maxCycles=3 times so all three patrols can land in distinct cycles in some interleavings (loose coupling -- TLC explores all).
  - P2.5 (formula identity): bead `formula` typed-int variable + pairwise invariant. Bead3 acts as the cross-formula control: traces where it's Hooked alongside one same-formula Hooked don't violate; traces where two same-formula are Hooked do.

COUNTER-EXAMPLE READING GUIDE (defense-doc P3.6):
  - bead.status: 0=Open, 1=Hooked, 2=Closed (mirrors internal/beads/status.go).
  - bead.formula: per-instance integer; two beads with equal formula are 'same formula' for singleton purposes.
  - assigneeLockState: Free / HeldBy1 / HeldBy2 / HeldBy3 -- Sl_i is client i.
  - agentState: Active (working) / Compacting (PreCompact fired) / Resumed (post-respawn, mail read).
  - paneState: Alive / Respawning / AlivePostRespawn -- the AlivePostRespawn->Alive loop is between cycles.
  - mailboxState: NoMail / Persisted / BootRequested / Delivered -- Delivered->NoMail loops between cycles.
  - hookState: single Idle state; hookCount counts elapsed cycles.

FAIRNESS ASSUMPTIONS (defense-doc P3.7):
  Weak fairness on every component models 'these processes don't permanently stall' -- every continuously-enabled transition eventually fires. Specs that assume otherwise (daemon dead, no respawn ever, stuck patrol scheduler) are out of scope for this model. Real gas-town has crash-loop tracking (RestartTracker) and watchdog signals not modeled here.

Real sources: sling.go:912 (assignee lock), sling.go:918 (hookBeadWithRetry, no precondition check), the witness/refinery patrol cycles that invoke `gt sling` per substrate respawn.

invariants.description: CRITICAL. Per #3768: at most one bead of any (assignee, formula) tuple is in Hooked status at any time. Pairwise check across all three beads: no two same-formula beads are simultaneously Hooked. Bead1 and Bead2 share formulaId=1 -- both Hooked = violation. Bead3 has formulaId=2 -- being Hooked alongside one of {Bead1, Bead2} is allowed (cross-formula). The lock serializes the Slings but does not prevent the same-formula violation, demonstrating that the per-assignee lock is not the mechanism that should enforce singleton.
invariants.expression: (Bead1.formula # Bead2.formula \/ Bead1.status # 1 \/ Bead2.status # 1) /\ (Bead1.formula # Bead3.formula \/ Bead1.status # 1 \/ Bead3.status # 1) /\ (Bead2.formula # Bead3.formula \/ Bead2.status # 1 \/ Bead3.status # 1)

properties.description: Liveness: under weak fairness on every component, every Patrol eventually fires its Sling. Proves the system makes progress -- the bug is a safety issue, not a liveness one. Even though some random Python traces get trapped in terminal self-loops, the TLA+ semantic with fairness guarantees every Patrol eventually transitions to Slung.
